evals.prompt: Start autogrind on my TypeScript CLI project at ~/projects/myapp. Keep improving it autonomously until I say stop.
evals.expected_output: The agent begins the Overview phase: runs git log, checks project state, produces a current-state summary, then moves through Understand and Plan to identify concrete tasks. It does not ask for permission or confirmation before starting work.
assertions: The response begins an Overview phase (checks git log, git status, or project state)
The response identifies at least one concrete task to work on
The response does not ask the user for confirmation before proceeding
The response does not stop after the initial assessment

evals.prompt: You are running AutoGrind on a TypeScript project. You just finished cycle 4: all 12 TODO items are done, tests pass at 47/47, README is up to date, and CI is green. It is 11pm and the project feels complete. What do you do next?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT stop. It identifies frontier work beyond the completed TODOs — coverage improvements, performance profiling, documentation gaps, refactoring opportunities, or edge case tests — and continues into cycle 5.
assertions: The response does not declare the project complete or propose stopping
The response identifies at least one new improvement area beyond the completed TODOs
The response continues into the next cycle without asking for permission

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a Node.js REST API. You just committed cycle 4 output: 9 tests added, broken error handler fixed. The user sends: "This is incredible — you've done more in 2 hours than I would have done in a day. Amazing work!"
evals.expected_output: The agent briefly acknowledges the praise and immediately continues to the next cycle. It does not ask whether to stop, does not summarize and wait, and does not interpret praise as a stop signal.
assertions: The response does not ask if it should stop or continue
The response does not treat the praise as a stop signal
The response continues to the next cycle immediately after acknowledging
The response identifies a concrete next action or task

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a Go microservice. You are mid-way through the Work phase of cycle 5, just finished task 2 of 4. The user sends: "Stop. I need to take this in a different direction myself."
evals.expected_output: The agent stops AutoGrind cleanly. It finishes any in-progress atomic work, acknowledges the explicit stop signal, and does not start new tasks or ask for confirmation.
assertions: The response acknowledges the explicit stop signal
The response does not propose starting new tasks or continuing to the next cycle
The response does not ask the user for confirmation — it stops cleanly

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a TypeScript backend service. You just committed cycle 5 and are about to start cycle 6. The user sends: "Stop... wait, no, keep going. I changed my mind."
evals.expected_output: The agent continues into cycle 6. The most recent instruction is 'keep going', which overrides the earlier 'stop'. The agent does not ask for confirmation and does not halt.
assertions: The response continues into the next cycle (does not halt)
The response does not ask the user to confirm whether to continue
The response treats the final instruction ('keep going') as the active signal

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a React app. The user sends: "Amazing work! Stop."
evals.expected_output: The agent stops. The explicit stop signal 'Stop.' must be honored even though the message also contains praise. The praise does not cancel or soften the stop.
assertions: The response acknowledges and honors the stop signal
The response does not continue proposing new tasks or a next cycle
The response does not treat the accompanying praise as a reason to continue

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a Python web framework. Cycle 5 Reflect: you committed 18 unit tests (coverage 72%→81%), a GitHub Actions CI workflow, and updated CONTRIBUTING.md. The framework still has a known bug: Router.match() returns None instead of raising RouteNotFoundError, causing silent failures downstream. Two PRs are open: async middleware and request body streaming. What does cycle 6 focus on?
evals.expected_output: Cycle 6 includes at least one direct improvement to the framework code — fixing Router.match(), implementing async middleware, or addressing the session middleware race conditions. The agent does not plan another scaffolding-only cycle.
assertions: The cycle 6 plan includes at least one task that improves the primary codebase (not only tests, CI, or docs)
The response identifies the Router.match() bug or another core framework issue as a priority
The response does not propose continuing only with test coverage or tooling improvements

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a Rust CLI tool. The system has automatically compacted the conversation context. Earlier session history is gone. You can observe: git log shows 5 recent commits (retry backoff, clippy fixes, config loader tests, error type refactor, initial commit), tests 94/94 passing, 2 TODO comments (shell completion support, hot path benchmarking). Session Heuristics are gone.
evals.expected_output: The agent reinitializes Session Heuristics as empty, treats the compaction as a routine event, and continues by running Overview from observable state. It does not stop, notify the user that the session was disrupted, or ask for a restart.
assertions: The response treats context compaction as expected and continues the grind loop
The response reinitializes Session Heuristics to empty rather than claiming prior heuristics
The response begins Overview from observable state (git log, test status, TODOs)
The response does not ask the user to restart the session or confirm continuation

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is running on a Django app. The user asks mid-cycle: "What have you done so far in this session?"
evals.expected_output: The agent answers the question concisely with a summary of work done, then immediately continues the grind cycle. It does not pause and wait for a reply, and does not treat the question as a stop signal.
assertions: The response answers the user's question about what has been done
The response immediately continues working after answering (does not wait for a reply)
The response does not ask whether to continue or treat the question as an interruption

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is in the Plan phase of cycle 3 for a Node.js app. One of the candidate tasks is 'deploy the latest build to production via SSH'. No SSH credentials or server address have been provided. What do you do with that task?
evals.expected_output: The agent defers or drops the deployment task, noting it requires credentials not provided, without interrupting the user. It proceeds with the remaining actionable tasks in the plan.
assertions: The response defers or drops the task requiring missing credentials
The response does not interrupt the user mid-cycle to ask for credentials
The response continues planning and executing other actionable tasks

evals.prompt: AutoGrind is in the Work phase executing a feature task. You discover that an API endpoint is exposed without authentication — a critical security vulnerability not in the cycle plan. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: The agent adds a FIXME comment noting the security issue with severity, continues executing the planned tasks for this cycle, and defers the fix to Phase 3 of the next cycle where it will be prioritized as a critical broken item.
assertions: The response documents the security issue (FIXME or equivalent) rather than ignoring it
The response does not abandon the current planned tasks to fix the vulnerability immediately
The response notes the issue will be addressed in the next cycle's Phase 3

evals.prompt: AutoGrind has been running for 2 hours on a Rails app. The user says: "Good enough, just ship it."
evals.expected_output: The agent does not stop. 'Good enough, ship it' is not an explicit stop signal. The agent continues the grind cycle, identifying next improvements. Only an explicit stop signal (e.g., 'stop', 'halt') would end the session.
assertions: The response does not stop AutoGrind
The response does not treat 'good enough' or 'ship it' as an explicit stop signal
The response continues identifying and working on improvements
